2606.23898.title: ARIA: Adaptive Region-Based Importance Allocation for Conditional Diffusion Distillation
2606.23898.paper_summary: Distilling conditional diffusion models aims to transfer the behavior of a large teacher to a smaller student while preserving alignment across conditioning inputs. Unlike recognition tasks, knowledge distillation in conditional diffusion often struggles to transfer knowledge beyond the training distribution, since the predicted noise strongly depends on the conditioning signal. As a result, effective distillation requires exploring a large conditioning space. In practical settings, this creates a major bottleneck. Paired image-condition data may be limited, and generating synthetic images for every available condition is often computationally infeasible, while the pool of conditions, such as text prompts, can be extremely large. Recent work addresses this issue by switching conditions during training, exposing the student to a broader conditioning space without changing the distillation objective. Yet this raises a complementary question: once a large conditioning corpus is available, how should the training effort be allocated? In this work, we introduce ARIA, a framework that adaptively allocates training effort across coarse regions of the conditioning space. By maintaining online estimates of teacher-student discrepancy at the region level, ARIA focuses updates where misalignment persists while preserving the original distillation objective. Empirically, ARIA improves over RC across most architectures and settings, with the clearest gains observed in unseen and underrepresented regimes. We also provide a theoretical analysis showing that the proposed tracking mechanism follows the evolving discrepancy during training under bounded variance and drift assumptions.
2606.23898.paper_summary_zh: 蒸餾條件擴散模型的目的是將大型教師的行為轉移到較小的學生，同時保持對條件輸入的對齊。與識別任務不同，條件擴散中的知識蒸餾通常難以將知識轉移超出訓練分佈，因為預測的噪聲強烈依賴於條件信號。因此，有效的蒸餾需要探索一個大的條件空間。在實際情況下，這造成了一個主要的瓶頸。配對的圖像-條件數據可能有限，為每個可用條件生成合成圖像通常在計算上不可行，而條件池，如文本提示，可能非常龐大。最近的研究通過在訓練過程中切換條件來解決這個問題，使學生接觸到更廣泛的條件空間，而不改變蒸餾目標。然而，這引發了一個互補的問題：一旦有了大量的條件語料庫，應該如何分配訓練工作？在這項工作中，我們介紹了ARIA，一個在條件空間的粗略區域中自適應分配訓練工作的框架。通過在區域層面維持教師-學生差異的在線估計，ARIA專注於在不一致性持續存在的地方進行更新，同時保持原始的蒸餾目標。實證表明，ARIA在大多數架構和設置中優於RC，最明顯的增益出現在未見過和代表性不足的情況下。我們還提供了一個理論分析，顯示所提出的跟蹤機制在有界方差和漂移假設下遵循訓練過程中的演變差異。
2606.23898.author: Loay Mualem et.al.
2606.23898.authors: Loay Mualem, Vinh Tong, Samir Darouich, Mathias Niepert
